title: AcyclicityUnderIntervention
lean_code_with_comments:
/-- claim_3_13 part A: if `G` is acyclic and `W ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V`, then
`G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W hW` is acyclic. Mirrors the
acyclicity half of the `\Rem` immediately after def_3_13 in
`lecture-notes/lecture_notes/graphs.tex` (lines 821 -- 829).

The mathematical content is that every edge of the extended graph
has target `Sum.inl _` (`mem_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_E`:
either it is a `Sum.inl √ó Sum.inl`-lift of some `(v', w') ‚ąą G.E`, or
it is a fresh `Sum.inr ‚ü®w, _‚ü© ‚Üí Sum.inl w`, so the target is
`Sum.inl _` in both cases), so a directed cycle cannot pass through
a `Sum.inr` vertex -- there is no edge into a `Sum.inr` vertex. The
cycle therefore lives entirely in `Sum.inl`-lifted edges and
projects to a directed cycle in `G` of the same length,
contradicting `G.IsAcyclic`. See the per-theorem comment block above
for the walk-lifting plan; the precondition `hW : W ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V`
appears for *typing* reasons only (it is the well-typedness
condition of `extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes`), not as a
mathematical hypothesis of acyclicity preservation.

`G` and `W` are implicit because they are unifiable from `hW` and
from the conclusion; `hW` is explicit because
`extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes` requires it explicitly at every
call site; `h : G.IsAcyclic` is the mathematical hypothesis under
transport. Naming follows Mathlib's `<conclusion>_<construction>`
convention, consistent with `isAcyclic_hardInterventionOn`
(claim_3_3 part A) and `isAcyclic_nodeSplittingOn` (claim_3_6 part
B). -/
theorem isAcyclic_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes
    {G : CDMG őĪ} {W : Set őĪ} (hW : W ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V) (h : G.IsAcyclic) :
    (G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W hW).IsAcyclic := by
  -- Mirrors `tex/claim_3_13_proof_AcyclicityUnderIntervention.tex` Part A.
  -- Step 1: a cycle's start vertex `v` is the target of its closing step, so
  -- by `extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_target_isInl` it is
  -- `Sum.inl _`. Step 2: lift the cycle to a directed walk in `G` of the
  -- same length via `walkLiftExtendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes`.
  -- Step 3: contradict `G.IsAcyclic` at the projected vertex.
  rintro v _hv ‚ü®ŌÄ, h_dir, h_pos‚ü©
  -- The end vertex `v` of the cycle is the target of the closing step,
  -- hence `Sum.inl _` by `walkEnd_isInl`.
  obtain ‚ü®v', hv'‚ü© := walkEnd_isInl ŌÄ h_dir h_pos
  subst hv'
  -- Lift the whole cycle to a directed walk in `G` of the same length.
  obtain ‚ü®ŌĀ, h_ŌĀ_dir, h_ŌĀ_len‚ü© :=
    walkLiftExtendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes hW ŌÄ h_dir v' v' rfl rfl
  have hŌĀ_pos : 1 ‚Č§ ŌĀ.length := by rw [h_ŌĀ_len]; exact h_pos
  -- `v' ‚ąą G` follows from the first step of `ŌÄ`: by piece-1 of
  -- `mem_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_E`, the underlying edge
  -- `(v', x) ‚ąą G.E` puts `v' ‚ąą G` via `G.E_subset`.
  have hv'_G : v' ‚ąą G := by
    cases ŌÄ with
    | nil _ => simp at h_pos
    | @cons _ m _ s _ =>
      cases s with
      | forward he =>
        have he_ext : (Sum.inl v', m) ‚ąą
            (G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W hW).E := he
        rw [mem_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_E] at he_ext
        rcases he_ext with ‚ü®v‚āĀ, _, hE, h_eq‚ü© | ‚ü®w', h_eq‚ü©
        ¬∑ rw [Prod.mk.injEq] at h_eq
          have hv_eq : v' = v‚āĀ := Sum.inl_injective h_eq.1
          subst hv_eq
          exact CDMG.mem_iff.mpr (Set.mem_prod.mp (G.E_subset hE)).1
        ¬∑ rw [Prod.mk.injEq] at h_eq
          exact nomatch h_eq.1
      | backward _ => simp at h_dir
      | bidir _ => simp at h_dir
  exact h v' hv'_G ‚ü®ŌĀ, h_ŌĀ_dir, hŌĀ_pos‚ü©

/-- The *extension order* `extensionOrder r` on the carrier `őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎS`,
induced by a base relation `r : őĪ ‚Üí őĪ ‚Üí Prop` and a parameter
set `S : Set őĪ`. This is the LN's "putting all `I_w` nodes first"
recipe from claim_3_13: every `Sum.inr ‚ü®w, _‚ü©` precedes every
`Sum.inl v`, and same-side pairs inherit `r` from the underlying
`őĪ`-values. The four cases of the pattern match correspond to the
four `(Sum.inl/inr, Sum.inl/inr)` corner pairs; see the design
block above and the file-level docstring.

`S` is implicit because it is recovered from the relation's domain
type `őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎS` at the call site; in
`isTopologicalOrder_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_extend` we
spell it `extensionOrder (S := W \ G.J) r` to make the choice
explicit. `noncomputable` because we do not assume any decidability
on `r`; downstream uses are all `Prop`-valued so the classical
choice has no observable cost. Used by
`isTopologicalOrder_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_extend`
(this file) and quoted by name in claim_3_14 / claim_3_15
(commutation) and the iSCM / do-calculus chapters that recurse
along a topological order of an extended CDMG. -/
noncomputable def extensionOrder {S : Set őĪ} (r : őĪ ‚Üí őĪ ‚Üí Prop) :
    (őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎS) ‚Üí (őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎS) ‚Üí Prop
  | Sum.inl v‚āĀ, Sum.inl v‚āā => r v‚āĀ v‚āā
  | Sum.inr ‚ü®w‚āĀ, _‚ü©, Sum.inr ‚ü®w‚āā, _‚ü© => r w‚āĀ w‚āā
  | Sum.inr _, Sum.inl _ => True
  | Sum.inl _, Sum.inr _ => False

/-- claim_3_13 part C: if `W ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V` and `r` is a topological
order of `G`, then `extensionOrder (S := W \ G.J) r` is a topological
order of `G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W hW`. Mirrors the
constructive extension direction of the `\Rem` immediately after
def_3_13 in `lecture-notes/lecture_notes/graphs.tex` (lines 821 --
829), with the LN's "put all the `I_w` nodes first" recipe encoded
as the four-case `extensionOrder` pattern match.

The `W ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V` precondition is structurally required by
`extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes` itself (def_3_13) -- the fresh
edge `Sum.inr ‚ü®w, _‚ü© ‚Üí Sum.inl w` needs its target `Sum.inl w` to
live in the extended graph's `V`-set, which is true only when
`w ‚ąą G.V` for `w ‚ąą W \ G.J`. This is not a topological-order
condition; it is the def_3_13 condition transported.

See `isTopologicalOrder_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_restrict`
below for the inverse-direction companion (a topological order of
the extended graph restricts to one of `G`),
`isAcyclic_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes` above for the
acyclicity half, and the file-level docstring for the rationale
behind splitting the LN's single `\Rem` into three theorems plus
one named construction. -/
theorem isTopologicalOrder_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_extend
    {G : CDMG őĪ} {W : Set őĪ} (hW : W ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V)
    {r : őĪ ‚Üí őĪ ‚Üí Prop} (hr : G.IsTopologicalOrder r) :
    (G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W hW).IsTopologicalOrder
      (extensionOrder (S := W \ G.J) r) := by
  -- Mirrors `tex/claim_3_13_proof_AcyclicityUnderIntervention.tex` Part C.
  -- The four `IsTopologicalOrder` fields discharge by case-splitting on the
  -- `Sum.inl/inr` carrier; same-side cases reduce to `hr`-fields on
  -- underlying `őĪ`-values, cross cases land in the `True`/`False` slots
  -- of `extensionOrder`. `parent_lt` for piece-2 fresh edges lands
  -- directly in the cross-case `True` slot, no `hr` needed.
  -- Helper: `Sum.inl v ‚ąą G.ext W hW ‚ÜĒ v ‚ąą G`.
  have mem_inl : ‚ąÄ {v : őĪ},
      (Sum.inl v : őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎ(W \ G.J)) ‚ąą
        G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W hW ‚ÜĒ v ‚ąą G := by
    intro v
    simp only [CDMG.mem_iff, extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_J,
      extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_V, Set.mem_union, Set.mem_image,
      Set.mem_range]
    constructor
    ¬∑ rintro ((‚ü®j, hj, hjv‚ü© | ‚ü®w, hw‚ü©) | ‚ü®v', hv', hvv'‚ü©)
      ¬∑ cases Sum.inl_injective hjv; exact Or.inl hj
      ¬∑ exact nomatch hw
      ¬∑ cases Sum.inl_injective hvv'; exact Or.inr hv'
    ¬∑ rintro (hJ | hV)
      ¬∑ exact Or.inl (Or.inl ‚ü®v, hJ, rfl‚ü©)
      ¬∑ exact Or.inr ‚ü®v, hV, rfl‚ü©
  -- Helper: every fresh `w' : ‚ÜĎ(W \ G.J)` underlies a node of `G`.
  -- (`w' ‚ąą W` via `hW : W ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V`; `w' ‚ąČ G.J` forces `w' ‚ąą G.V`.)
  have inr_mem_G : ‚ąÄ w' : ‚ÜĎ(W \ G.J), (w' : őĪ) ‚ąą G := by
    intro w'
    have hwW : (w' : őĪ) ‚ąą W := w'.property.1
    have hwJ : (w' : őĪ) ‚ąČ G.J := w'.property.2
    rcases hW hwW with hJ | hV
    ¬∑ exact absurd hJ hwJ
    ¬∑ exact Or.inr hV
  refine ‚ü®?_, ?_, ?_, ?_‚ü©
  ¬∑ -- irrefl: case-split on the carrier; both branches collapse
    -- `extensionOrder r v v` to `r v' v'` for `v' ‚ąą G`.
    rintro (v | ‚ü®w, hw‚ü©) hv
    ¬∑ exact hr.irrefl v (mem_inl.mp hv)
    ¬∑ exact hr.irrefl (w : őĪ) (inr_mem_G ‚ü®w, hw‚ü©)
  ¬∑ -- trans: case-split on all three carrier shapes (8 cases). The cross
    -- cases `O ‚Üí F` are `False`, so any case using such a step as a premise
    -- is vacuous. The remaining cases collapse to `hr.trans` on the
    -- underlying `őĪ`-values; the `F ‚Üí O` cross slot is `True` and absorbs.
    rintro (a | ‚ü®a, ha‚ü©) h_a (b | ‚ü®b, hb‚ü©) h_b (c | ‚ü®c, hc‚ü©) h_c
    ¬∑ -- (inl a, inl b, inl c)
      intro h_ab h_bc
      exact hr.trans a (mem_inl.mp h_a) b (mem_inl.mp h_b) c (mem_inl.mp h_c)
        h_ab h_bc
    ¬∑ -- (inl a, inl b, inr c): h_bc : extensionOrder r (inl b) (inr c) = False
      intro _h_ab h_bc
      exact h_bc.elim
    ¬∑ -- (inl a, inr b, inl c): h_ab : extensionOrder r (inl a) (inr b) = False
      intro h_ab _
      exact h_ab.elim
    ¬∑ -- (inl a, inr b, inr c): h_ab : extensionOrder r (inl a) (inr b) = False
      intro h_ab _
      exact h_ab.elim
    ¬∑ -- (inr a, inl b, inl c): h_ab : extensionOrder r (inr a) (inl b) = True;
      -- conclusion: extensionOrder r (inr a) (inl c) = True.
      intro _ _
      trivial
    ¬∑ -- (inr a, inl b, inr c): h_bc : extensionOrder r (inl b) (inr c) = False
      intro _h_ab h_bc
      exact h_bc.elim
    ¬∑ -- (inr a, inr b, inl c): conclusion is the cross `True` slot.
      intro _ _
      trivial
    ¬∑ -- (inr a, inr b, inr c): all three fresh; reduces to `hr.trans` on `(őĪ)`.
      intro h_ab h_bc
      exact hr.trans (a : őĪ) (inr_mem_G ‚ü®a, ha‚ü©) b (inr_mem_G ‚ü®b, hb‚ü©)
        c (inr_mem_G ‚ü®c, hc‚ü©) h_ab h_bc
  ¬∑ -- trichotomous: case-split on both endpoints (4 cases). Each same-side
    -- case reduces to `hr.trichotomous` on the underlying `őĪ`-values; cross
    -- cases land in the `True` slot.
    rintro (a | ‚ü®a, ha‚ü©) h_a (b | ‚ü®b, hb‚ü©) h_b
    ¬∑ -- (inl a, inl b)
      rcases hr.trichotomous a (mem_inl.mp h_a) b (mem_inl.mp h_b) with
        h | rfl | h
      ¬∑ exact Or.inl h
      ¬∑ exact Or.inr (Or.inl rfl)
      ¬∑ exact Or.inr (Or.inr h)
    ¬∑ -- (inl a, inr b): the cross slot `(inl, inr)` is False, so the only
      -- live disjuncts are middle `=` (impossible by constructor) and the
      -- `(inr, inl)` `True` slot.
      exact Or.inr (Or.inr trivial)
    ¬∑ -- (inr a, inl b): the cross slot `(inr, inl)` is True.
      exact Or.inl trivial
    ¬∑ -- (inr a, inr b)
      rcases hr.trichotomous (a : őĪ) (inr_mem_G ‚ü®a, ha‚ü©) (b : őĪ)
        (inr_mem_G ‚ü®b, hb‚ü©) with h | h_eq | h
      ¬∑ exact Or.inl h
      ¬∑ refine Or.inr (Or.inl ?_)
        exact congrArg Sum.inr (Subtype.ext h_eq)
      ¬∑ exact Or.inr (Or.inr h)
  ¬∑ -- parent_lt: case-split on the two pieces of
    -- `mem_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_E`.
    intro v w h_pa
    obtain ‚ü®_, h_vw_E‚ü© := h_pa
    change (v, w) ‚ąą (G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W hW).E at h_vw_E
    rw [mem_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_E] at h_vw_E
    rcases h_vw_E with ‚ü®v‚āĀ, v‚āā, hE, h_eq‚ü© | ‚ü®w', h_eq‚ü©
    ¬∑ -- Piece 1: (v, w) = (Sum.inl v‚āĀ, Sum.inl v‚āā) with (v‚āĀ, v‚āā) ‚ąą G.E.
      -- `extensionOrder` fires the `(inl, inl)` case, reducing to `hr.parent_lt`.
      rw [Prod.mk.injEq] at h_eq
      obtain ‚ü®hv_eq, hw_eq‚ü© := h_eq
      subst hv_eq
      subst hw_eq
      have hv‚āĀ_G : v‚āĀ ‚ąą G :=
        CDMG.mem_iff.mpr (Set.mem_prod.mp (G.E_subset hE)).1
      exact hr.parent_lt ‚ü®hv‚āĀ_G, hE‚ü©
    ¬∑ -- Piece 2: (v, w) = (Sum.inr w', Sum.inl w'.val). The `(inr, inl)`
      -- cross case of `extensionOrder` fires with `True` -- no `hr` needed.
      rw [Prod.mk.injEq] at h_eq
      obtain ‚ü®hv_eq, hw_eq‚ü© := h_eq
      subst hv_eq
      subst hw_eq
      trivial

/-- claim_3_13 part B: if `W ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V` and `r` is a topological
order of `G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W hW`, then the
restriction `fun v w => r (Sum.inl v) (Sum.inl w)` is a topological
order of `G`. Mirrors the restriction direction of the `\Rem`
immediately after def_3_13 in
`lecture-notes/lecture_notes/graphs.tex` (lines 821 -- 829, first
sentence second clause: "a topological order for `G_{\doit(I_W)}` is
also one for `G`").

The `W ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V` precondition is structurally required by
`extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes` itself (def_3_13), not by the
restriction direction per se; see
`isTopologicalOrder_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_extend` for
the matching extension direction and the file-level docstring for
the rationale behind splitting the LN's single `\Rem` into three
theorems plus one named construction.

Inverse-direction companion to the extension theorem: field-by-field
transport using the fact that `Sum.inl` embedding takes `G`'s nodes
into the extended graph's nodes
(`extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_J` /
`extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_V` make this precise) and
`Sum.inl`-images of `G.E` are edges of the extended graph
(`mem_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_E` first disjunct). -/
theorem isTopologicalOrder_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_restrict
    {G : CDMG őĪ} {W : Set őĪ} (hW : W ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V)
    {r : (őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎ(W \ G.J)) ‚Üí (őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎ(W \ G.J)) ‚Üí Prop}
    (hr : (G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W hW).IsTopologicalOrder r) :
    G.IsTopologicalOrder (fun v w => r (Sum.inl v) (Sum.inl w)) := by
  -- Mirrors `tex/claim_3_13_proof_AcyclicityUnderIntervention.tex` Part B.
  -- Inverse-direction companion to Part C: field-by-field transport
  -- applying `hr.{irrefl, trans, trichotomous}` at `Sum.inl`-lifted nodes
  -- (membership transported via `extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_J`
  -- / `_V`); `parent_lt` lifts `(v, w) ‚ąą G.E` to `(Sum.inl v, Sum.inl w)
  -- ‚ąą G_ext.E` via the first disjunct of
  -- `mem_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_E`.
  -- Helper: `Sum.inl v ‚ąą G.ext W hW` whenever `v ‚ąą G`.
  have mem_inl : ‚ąÄ {v : őĪ}, v ‚ąą G ‚Üí
      (Sum.inl v : őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎ(W \ G.J)) ‚ąą
        G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W hW := by
    intro v hv
    simp only [CDMG.mem_iff, extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_J,
      extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_V, Set.mem_union, Set.mem_image,
      Set.mem_range]
    rcases hv with hJ | hV
    ¬∑ exact Or.inl (Or.inl ‚ü®v, hJ, rfl‚ü©)
    ¬∑ exact Or.inr ‚ü®v, hV, rfl‚ü©
  refine ‚ü®?_, ?_, ?_, ?_‚ü©
  ¬∑ -- irrefl: transport `hr.irrefl` at `Sum.inl v`.
    intro v hv
    exact hr.irrefl (Sum.inl v) (mem_inl hv)
  ¬∑ -- trans: transport via `hr.trans` at the three `Sum.inl`-lifted vertices.
    intro v hv w hw x hx
    exact hr.trans (Sum.inl v) (mem_inl hv) (Sum.inl w) (mem_inl hw)
      (Sum.inl x) (mem_inl hx)
  ¬∑ -- trichotomous: transport via `hr.trichotomous`; the middle disjunct
    -- `Sum.inl v = Sum.inl w` collapses to `v = w` by `Sum.inl_injective`.
    intro v hv w hw
    rcases hr.trichotomous (Sum.inl v) (mem_inl hv) (Sum.inl w) (mem_inl hw)
      with h | h_eq | h
    ¬∑ exact Or.inl h
    ¬∑ exact Or.inr (Or.inl (Sum.inl_injective h_eq))
    ¬∑ exact Or.inr (Or.inr h)
  ¬∑ -- parent_lt: lift `(v, w) ‚ąą G.E` to a `Sum.inl √ó Sum.inl`-lifted edge
    -- of the extended graph via piece 1 of
    -- `mem_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_E`; then `hr.parent_lt`.
    intro v w h_pa
    obtain ‚ü®hv_G, h_vw_E‚ü© := h_pa
    have h_lifted : (Sum.inl v, Sum.inl w) ‚ąą
        (G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W hW).E := by
      rw [mem_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_E]
      exact Or.inl ‚ü®v, w, h_vw_E, rfl‚ü©
    exact hr.parent_lt ‚ü®mem_inl hv_G, h_lifted‚ü©
lean_code_without_comments:
theorem isAcyclic_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes
    {G : CDMG őĪ} {W : Set őĪ} (hW : W ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V) (h : G.IsAcyclic) :
    (G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W hW).IsAcyclic := by

  rintro v _hv ‚ü®ŌÄ, h_dir, h_pos‚ü©

  obtain ‚ü®v', hv'‚ü© := walkEnd_isInl ŌÄ h_dir h_pos
  subst hv'

  obtain ‚ü®ŌĀ, h_ŌĀ_dir, h_ŌĀ_len‚ü© :=
    walkLiftExtendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes hW ŌÄ h_dir v' v' rfl rfl
  have hŌĀ_pos : 1 ‚Č§ ŌĀ.length := by rw [h_ŌĀ_len]; exact h_pos

  have hv'_G : v' ‚ąą G := by
    cases ŌÄ with
    | nil _ => simp at h_pos
    | @cons _ m _ s _ =>
      cases s with
      | forward he =>
        have he_ext : (Sum.inl v', m) ‚ąą
            (G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W hW).E := he
        rw [mem_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_E] at he_ext
        rcases he_ext with ‚ü®v‚āĀ, _, hE, h_eq‚ü© | ‚ü®w', h_eq‚ü©
        ¬∑ rw [Prod.mk.injEq] at h_eq
          have hv_eq : v' = v‚āĀ := Sum.inl_injective h_eq.1
          subst hv_eq
          exact CDMG.mem_iff.mpr (Set.mem_prod.mp (G.E_subset hE)).1
        ¬∑ rw [Prod.mk.injEq] at h_eq
          exact nomatch h_eq.1
      | backward _ => simp at h_dir
      | bidir _ => simp at h_dir
  exact h v' hv'_G ‚ü®ŌĀ, h_ŌĀ_dir, hŌĀ_pos‚ü©

noncomputable def extensionOrder {S : Set őĪ} (r : őĪ ‚Üí őĪ ‚Üí Prop) :
    (őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎS) ‚Üí (őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎS) ‚Üí Prop
  | Sum.inl v‚āĀ, Sum.inl v‚āā => r v‚āĀ v‚āā
  | Sum.inr ‚ü®w‚āĀ, _‚ü©, Sum.inr ‚ü®w‚āā, _‚ü© => r w‚āĀ w‚āā
  | Sum.inr _, Sum.inl _ => True
  | Sum.inl _, Sum.inr _ => False

theorem isTopologicalOrder_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_extend
    {G : CDMG őĪ} {W : Set őĪ} (hW : W ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V)
    {r : őĪ ‚Üí őĪ ‚Üí Prop} (hr : G.IsTopologicalOrder r) :
    (G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W hW).IsTopologicalOrder
      (extensionOrder (S := W \ G.J) r) := by

  have mem_inl : ‚ąÄ {v : őĪ},
      (Sum.inl v : őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎ(W \ G.J)) ‚ąą
        G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W hW ‚ÜĒ v ‚ąą G := by
    intro v
    simp only [CDMG.mem_iff, extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_J,
      extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_V, Set.mem_union, Set.mem_image,
      Set.mem_range]
    constructor
    ¬∑ rintro ((‚ü®j, hj, hjv‚ü© | ‚ü®w, hw‚ü©) | ‚ü®v', hv', hvv'‚ü©)
      ¬∑ cases Sum.inl_injective hjv; exact Or.inl hj
      ¬∑ exact nomatch hw
      ¬∑ cases Sum.inl_injective hvv'; exact Or.inr hv'
    ¬∑ rintro (hJ | hV)
      ¬∑ exact Or.inl (Or.inl ‚ü®v, hJ, rfl‚ü©)
      ¬∑ exact Or.inr ‚ü®v, hV, rfl‚ü©

  have inr_mem_G : ‚ąÄ w' : ‚ÜĎ(W \ G.J), (w' : őĪ) ‚ąą G := by
    intro w'
    have hwW : (w' : őĪ) ‚ąą W := w'.property.1
    have hwJ : (w' : őĪ) ‚ąČ G.J := w'.property.2
    rcases hW hwW with hJ | hV
    ¬∑ exact absurd hJ hwJ
    ¬∑ exact Or.inr hV
  refine ‚ü®?_, ?_, ?_, ?_‚ü©
  ¬∑

    rintro (v | ‚ü®w, hw‚ü©) hv
    ¬∑ exact hr.irrefl v (mem_inl.mp hv)
    ¬∑ exact hr.irrefl (w : őĪ) (inr_mem_G ‚ü®w, hw‚ü©)
  ¬∑

    rintro (a | ‚ü®a, ha‚ü©) h_a (b | ‚ü®b, hb‚ü©) h_b (c | ‚ü®c, hc‚ü©) h_c
    ¬∑
      intro h_ab h_bc
      exact hr.trans a (mem_inl.mp h_a) b (mem_inl.mp h_b) c (mem_inl.mp h_c)
        h_ab h_bc
    ¬∑
      intro _h_ab h_bc
      exact h_bc.elim
    ¬∑
      intro h_ab _
      exact h_ab.elim
    ¬∑
      intro h_ab _
      exact h_ab.elim
    ¬∑

      intro _ _
      trivial
    ¬∑
      intro _h_ab h_bc
      exact h_bc.elim
    ¬∑
      intro _ _
      trivial
    ¬∑
      intro h_ab h_bc
      exact hr.trans (a : őĪ) (inr_mem_G ‚ü®a, ha‚ü©) b (inr_mem_G ‚ü®b, hb‚ü©)
        c (inr_mem_G ‚ü®c, hc‚ü©) h_ab h_bc
  ¬∑

    rintro (a | ‚ü®a, ha‚ü©) h_a (b | ‚ü®b, hb‚ü©) h_b
    ¬∑
      rcases hr.trichotomous a (mem_inl.mp h_a) b (mem_inl.mp h_b) with
        h | rfl | h
      ¬∑ exact Or.inl h
      ¬∑ exact Or.inr (Or.inl rfl)
      ¬∑ exact Or.inr (Or.inr h)
    ¬∑

      exact Or.inr (Or.inr trivial)
    ¬∑
      exact Or.inl trivial
    ¬∑
      rcases hr.trichotomous (a : őĪ) (inr_mem_G ‚ü®a, ha‚ü©) (b : őĪ)
        (inr_mem_G ‚ü®b, hb‚ü©) with h | h_eq | h
      ¬∑ exact Or.inl h
      ¬∑ refine Or.inr (Or.inl ?_)
        exact congrArg Sum.inr (Subtype.ext h_eq)
      ¬∑ exact Or.inr (Or.inr h)
  ¬∑

    intro v w h_pa
    obtain ‚ü®_, h_vw_E‚ü© := h_pa
    change (v, w) ‚ąą (G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W hW).E at h_vw_E
    rw [mem_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_E] at h_vw_E
    rcases h_vw_E with ‚ü®v‚āĀ, v‚āā, hE, h_eq‚ü© | ‚ü®w', h_eq‚ü©
    ¬∑

      rw [Prod.mk.injEq] at h_eq
      obtain ‚ü®hv_eq, hw_eq‚ü© := h_eq
      subst hv_eq
      subst hw_eq
      have hv‚āĀ_G : v‚āĀ ‚ąą G :=
        CDMG.mem_iff.mpr (Set.mem_prod.mp (G.E_subset hE)).1
      exact hr.parent_lt ‚ü®hv‚āĀ_G, hE‚ü©
    ¬∑

      rw [Prod.mk.injEq] at h_eq
      obtain ‚ü®hv_eq, hw_eq‚ü© := h_eq
      subst hv_eq
      subst hw_eq
      trivial

theorem isTopologicalOrder_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_restrict
    {G : CDMG őĪ} {W : Set őĪ} (hW : W ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V)
    {r : (őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎ(W \ G.J)) ‚Üí (őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎ(W \ G.J)) ‚Üí Prop}
    (hr : (G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W hW).IsTopologicalOrder r) :
    G.IsTopologicalOrder (fun v w => r (Sum.inl v) (Sum.inl w)) := by

  have mem_inl : ‚ąÄ {v : őĪ}, v ‚ąą G ‚Üí
      (Sum.inl v : őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎ(W \ G.J)) ‚ąą
        G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W hW := by
    intro v hv
    simp only [CDMG.mem_iff, extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_J,
      extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_V, Set.mem_union, Set.mem_image,
      Set.mem_range]
    rcases hv with hJ | hV
    ¬∑ exact Or.inl (Or.inl ‚ü®v, hJ, rfl‚ü©)
    ¬∑ exact Or.inr ‚ü®v, hV, rfl‚ü©
  refine ‚ü®?_, ?_, ?_, ?_‚ü©
  ¬∑
    intro v hv
    exact hr.irrefl (Sum.inl v) (mem_inl hv)
  ¬∑
    intro v hv w hw x hx
    exact hr.trans (Sum.inl v) (mem_inl hv) (Sum.inl w) (mem_inl hw)
      (Sum.inl x) (mem_inl hx)
  ¬∑

    intro v hv w hw
    rcases hr.trichotomous (Sum.inl v) (mem_inl hv) (Sum.inl w) (mem_inl hw)
      with h | h_eq | h
    ¬∑ exact Or.inl h
    ¬∑ exact Or.inr (Or.inl (Sum.inl_injective h_eq))
    ¬∑ exact Or.inr (Or.inr h)
  ¬∑

    intro v w h_pa
    obtain ‚ü®hv_G, h_vw_E‚ü© := h_pa
    have h_lifted : (Sum.inl v, Sum.inl w) ‚ąą
        (G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W hW).E := by
      rw [mem_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_E]
      exact Or.inl ‚ü®v, w, h_vw_E, rfl‚ü©
    exact hr.parent_lt ‚ü®mem_inl hv_G, h_lifted‚ü©
lean_explanation:
The LN packs a three-statement remark into a single `\Rem` block: acyclicity preservation, plus the restriction and extension directions of "a topological order of one graph is a topological order of the other". The Lean file splits this into three theorems plus one named construction, all in the `Causality.CDMG` namespace.

Across the file, `őĪ : Type*` is the ambient vertex type ‚ÄĒ the LN's silent assumption that $G$ lives over some unspecified carrier. `G : CDMG őĪ` is the LN's $G = (J, V, E, L)$, with structure projections `G.J`, `G.V`, `G.E`, `G.L`. The LN's intervention-target set $W$ is `W : Set őĪ`, and the precondition $W \subseteq J \cup V$ is `hW : W ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V`, a `def_3_13` well-typedness condition that pins each fresh edge's target into the extended graph's `V`-set. The carrier of the extended graph is the tagged disjoint union `őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎ(W \ G.J)`: `Sum.inl v` corresponds to an original node, while `Sum.inr ‚ü®w, _‚ü©` corresponds to the fresh $I_w$ intervention node, indexed by the subtype `‚ÜĎ(W \ G.J)` of $W$-vertices not already in $G$'s inputs.

A handful of Mathlib idioms drive the encoding. `Sum.inl` and `Sum.inr` are the canonical injections into the disjoint union, and `Sum.inl_injective` is invoked to invert. The subtype `‚ÜĎ(W \ G.J) = {x : őĪ // x ‚ąą W \ G.J}` carries each fresh index together with a membership proof, with field accessors `w'.property.1 : (w' : őĪ) ‚ąą W` and `w'.property.2 : (w' : őĪ) ‚ąČ G.J`. `IsAcyclic` and `IsTopologicalOrder` are structures from `Section3_1`; the latter bundles four fields `irrefl`, `trans`, `trichotomous`, and `parent_lt`. The acyclicity argument inspects the inductive structure of `Walk` ‚ÄĒ its `nil`/`cons` shape, the three step constructors `Walk.forward`/`Walk.backward`/`Walk.bidir`, and the `Walk.IsDirected` predicate. The `@[simp]` characterisation `mem_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_E` makes the edge case-split tractable: every edge is either a piece-1 `Sum.inl √ó Sum.inl`-lift of some $(v_1, v_2) \in G.E$ or a piece-2 fresh edge from `Sum.inr ‚ü®w, _‚ü©` to `Sum.inl w`.

The acyclicity theorem takes `hW` and `h : G.IsAcyclic` and concludes that the extended graph is acyclic; three private helpers (excluded from the anchors) lift the directed-cycle argument by showing every edge of the extended graph has target `Sum.inl _`, every directed walk's intermediate vertices are `Sum.inl _`, and a directed walk from `Sum.inl v` to `Sum.inl w` lifts to a walk in $G$ of the same length. The named def `extensionOrder {S : Set őĪ} (r : őĪ ‚Üí őĪ ‚Üí Prop) : (őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎS) ‚Üí (őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎS) ‚Üí Prop` is the LN's "put all $I_w$ first" recipe, encoded as a four-case pattern match on the `Sum` carrier: same-side pairs inherit `r`, fresh-then-original is `True`, original-then-fresh is `False`. The extension theorem takes `hr : G.IsTopologicalOrder r` and concludes `(G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W hW).IsTopologicalOrder (extensionOrder (S := W \ G.J) r)`; the restriction theorem inverts via `fun v w => r (Sum.inl v) (Sum.inl w)` along the `Sum.inl` embedding.
design_choices:
**Splitting one `\Rem` into three theorems plus a named construction.** The LN bundles three distinct statements under one remark ‚ÄĒ acyclicity preservation, restriction of a topological order along the `Sum.inl` embedding, and extension via the "all $I_w$ first" recipe. Different downstream consumers need different halves: `claim_3_14` and `claim_3_15` (commutation with disjoint hard interventions and node-splitting) need acyclicity alone to discharge CADMG side conditions, while the iSCM and do-calculus chapters need the extension order named so they can recurse along it. The split is also *structurally forced* by the carrier change: `extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes` shifts the vertex type from `őĪ` to `őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎ(W \ G.J)`, so a topological order of $G$ and one of the extended graph live on different types and must be transported explicitly. The closest analogue, `claim_3_3` (hard intervention), keeps the carrier fixed and so collapses the two topological-order directions into a single relation; here the directions are genuinely two transports between distinct relation spaces, mirroring the parallel splitting in `claim_3_6` (node splitting).

**`extensionOrder` as a standalone named construction.** The LN's recipe is hedged with "e.g." ‚ÄĒ one specific extension, not the canonical one. Naming the relation lets downstream rows quote it by name rather than re-deriving the four-case match each time; mirrors `splitOrder` from `claim_3_6`. The parameter is named `S : Set őĪ` rather than hard-coding `W \ G.J`, so the relation composes with arbitrary subtypes; at the use site we spell `extensionOrder (S := W \ G.J) r` explicitly. The construction inspects no structure of $G$, so it takes no `G : CDMG őĪ` argument. It is `noncomputable` because `r` need not be decidable; downstream uses are `Prop`-valued.

**Cross cases as `True` / `False`.** The recipe is asymmetric: every fresh $I_w$ precedes every original node, never the other way. Encoding `(Sum.inr, Sum.inl) := True` and `(Sum.inl, Sum.inr) := False` makes the asymmetry directly visible and lets `trichotomous` and `trans` transport trivially on cross pairs. For `parent_lt`, the cross slot is exactly where the fresh edge from `Sum.inr ‚ü®w, _‚ü©` to `Sum.inl w` lands, so `True` discharges the obligation with no appeal to `hr`. The same-side `(Sum.inr, Sum.inr)` case inherits `r` from the underlying `őĪ`-values; the choice is free (no edges between fresh nodes), but inheriting `r` keeps the relation total, transitive, and irreflexive without a side relation on `‚ÜĎS`.

**Restriction as `fun v w => r (Sum.inl v) (Sum.inl w)`.** The `Sum.inl` embedding is the canonical lift of $G$'s carrier into the extended graph's carrier, so restricting `r` along it is the natural inverse of the `extensionOrder` recipe. This direction is a *Lean-only artefact* in the sense that the LN's "a topological order for $G_{\doit(I_W)}$ is also one for $G$" reads as a trivial syntactic remark when the carrier doesn't change (cf. `claim_3_3`). Because the carrier *does* change here, the sentence becomes substantive content: a literal `Sum.inl`-restriction must be performed to land in the right relation space, and naming it explicitly saves downstream consumers from re-deriving the transport.

**No `[Finite őĪ]` hypothesis; `hW` is typing-only.** Walk-lifting for acyclicity and the four `IsTopologicalOrder` fields all transport via the `@[simp]` membership lemmas of `ExtendingCDMGsWithInterventionNodes.lean`, none of which need finiteness. The precondition `hW : W ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V` is structurally required by `extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes` itself ‚ÄĒ it pins each fresh edge's target into the extended graph's `V`-set ‚ÄĒ but contributes nothing mathematically to acyclicity preservation, mirroring `isAcyclic_nodeSplittingOn` (`claim_3_6` part B).
